name: Dune du Pilat and Biscarosse beach - tour of the Etang de Cazaux and Sanguinet
name_original: Dune du Pilat et plage de Biscarosse - tour de l'Étang de Cazaux et de Sanguinet
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Biscarrosse traverse a landscape characterized by extensive pine forests, large freshwater lakes, and the Atlantic coastline. The region features a network of dedicated cycle paths, including sections of La Vélodyssée, Europe's longest coastal cycle route. Terrain is generally flat with some gentle undulations, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Biscarrosse The most popular touring cycling route is Dune du Pilat and Biscarosse beach - tour of the Etang de Cazaux and Sanguinet, a 40.7 miles (65.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route connects coastal features with the large freshwater lakes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Transaquitain Canal – Lake Cazaux-Sanguinet loop from Petit Étang de Biscarosse, a moderate 19.2 miles (31.0 km) path. This route offers scenic views around Lake Cazaux-Sanguinet, following the Transaquitain Canal. Local touring cyclists also love the Transaquitain Canal – View of the lake loop from Le Courbey, an 18.1 miles (29.2 km) trail leading through pine forests and offering lake views, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Biscarrosse is defined by serene lakes, vast pine forests, and coastal dunes, with an extensive network of cycle paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family rides to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Biscarrosse are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1400 reviews. More than 11000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Biscarrosse's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Biscarrosse?
A: Biscarrosse offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. The terrain is generally flat, characterized by extensive pine forests, large freshwater lakes like Lac de Biscarrosse and Lac de Cazaux-Sanguinet, and the Atlantic coastline. While mostly flat, you might encounter some gentle undulations, especially closer to the dunes or along certain forest paths. The region's dedicated cycle path network, including sections of La Vélodyssée, ensures smooth riding surfaces for most routes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Biscarrosse?
A: Yes, Biscarrosse is very family-friendly for cycling. Over 55 easy touring cycling routes are available. Many paths wind through shaded forests or skirt the calm lakes, offering safe and pleasant rides. For example, routes like the one from Navarrosse to Sanguinet (12.5 km) are ideal for families. A shorter, 7 km loop around Biscarrosse Plage is also perfect for a relaxed family outing or accessing local amenities.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Biscarrosse?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Biscarrosse offers over 20 difficult routes. These longer routes often combine different landscapes, such as coastal sections, forest trails, and lake circuits. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968107277/dune-du-pilat-and-biscarosse-beach-tour-of-the-etang-de-cazaux-and-sanguinet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dune du Pilat and Biscarosse beach - tour of the Etang de Cazaux and Sanguinet&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 65 km and connects coastal features with the large freshwater lakes. Another option is the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26417225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towards Biscarosse Beach – Mimizan cycle path loop from Biscarrosse&lt;/a&gt;, which stretches over 129 km.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around the lakes in Biscarrosse?
A: Absolutely. The Biscarrosse region is well-suited for circular lake tours. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29357411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transaquitain Canal – Lake Cazaux-Sanguinet loop from Petit Étang de Biscarosse&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic views around Lake Cazaux-Sanguinet, following the Transaquitain Canal. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41646390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transaquitain Canal – View of the lake loop from Le Courbey&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through pine forests and provides beautiful lake vistas.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Biscarrosse?
A: Touring cycling routes in Biscarrosse offer a wealth of natural beauty and points of interest. You'll encounter the serene freshwater lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Little Pond of Biscarrosse&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/950820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biscarrosse and Parentis Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/508229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cazaux-Sanguinet&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/871375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transaquitain Canal&lt;/a&gt;, which connects these lakes. The extensive Landes forest provides shaded paths, while coastal routes offer views of the Atlantic Ocean and sandy beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4776963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanguinet Lake Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Biscarrosse?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Biscarrosse is typically spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is pleasantly mild, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. The summer months (July-August) can be warm, but the extensive pine forests provide ample shade, and the lakes offer refreshing swimming opportunities. The dedicated cycle path network is well-maintained and accessible throughout most of the year.

Q: Are there dedicated cycle paths in Biscarrosse?
A: Yes, Biscarrosse is renowned for its exceptional network of dedicated cycle paths, totaling 145 km. These paths link the different villages, lakes, and the ocean, allowing cyclists to explore the region safely and comfortably. Biscarrosse is also a significant point on La Vélodyssée, Europe's longest cycle route, which runs along the Atlantic coast and offers a wild and invigorating course through the area.

Q: Where can I park my car if I want to start a touring cycling route around Biscarrosse?
A: Parking is generally available at various points around the lakes and near the main towns. For routes starting near the lakes, you can often find parking facilities close to popular access points or beaches, such as around the Lac de Biscarrosse or Lac de Cazaux-Sanguinet. In Biscarrosse Plage, there are also parking options available, especially outside of peak season. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Biscarrosse?
A: The touring cycling routes around Biscarrosse are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1400 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the tranquil pine forests and serene lakes to the invigorating Atlantic coastline. The extensive and well-maintained network of dedicated cycle paths, including sections of La Vélodyssée, is frequently highlighted as a major advantage, making it easy and safe to explore the area.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that connect Biscarrosse Plage to other towns or attractions?
A: Yes, there are several routes that connect Biscarrosse Plage to other notable areas. A popular section of La Vélodyssée runs from Biscarrosse Plage to Parentis-en-Born, covering about 33 kilometers and winding through dunes and skirting the Biscarrosse lakes. Another coastal route of approximately 20 kilometers connects Biscarrosse Plage to the iconic Dune du Pilat, offering a car-free way to visit Europe's highest dune with views of the forest and ocean beaches.

Q: Can I cycle along the Transaquitain Canal in Biscarrosse?
A: Yes, the Transaquitain Canal is a prominent feature in the Biscarrosse cycling landscape, connecting the region's large freshwater lakes. Many touring cycling routes utilize paths alongside the canal, offering peaceful and scenic rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7011953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transaquitain Canal – Lake Cazaux-Sanguinet loop from Biscarrosse plage les Viviers&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33.9 km route that follows the canal and provides beautiful views of the lake.
tips: The Pilat dune is the highest in Europe. Despite its size, it is fragile. The climb is worth it! The bravest don't take the stairs: the sand will heat up your thighs and calves! The view at its top is breathtaking.
White sand beach on the Lac de Cazaux, with a small refreshment bar.
Nice place to change a bit from the coastal beaches, even if there is a fairly busy sandy beach on the Cazaux pond.
Lovely ride on the wide promenade along the beach.
